task: push the red button to turn off the red light
raw:
The scene shows a robotic arm in a kitchen environment with a white cabinet, a cardboard box, and a white plastic container on the floor. The task is to push a red button to turn off a red light. Since the red light and button are not visible in the image, we assume they are on a control panel or appliance within the scene. The strategy involves reaching the control panel, identifying the red button, and pushing it. Failure modes include misidentifying the button or failing to apply sufficient force. The affordance reasoning focuses on the control panel's surface for interaction.

Goal: red_light_off; stable(gripper)
Scene: robotic_arm, white_cabinet, cardboard_box, white_plastic_container, control_panel
Step 1: reach(control_panel)
  affordance_region: the surface of the control_panel
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, control_panel, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, control_panel) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(control_panel)
  affordance_region: the surface of the control_panel
  contact: holding(control_panel) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(control_panel)
Step 3: push(red_button -> control_panel)
  affordance_region: the red_button on the control_panel
  contact: button_contact(red_button) [completion]; released(red_button) [completion]
  pose: stable(gripper)
  done_when: button_contact(red_button) AND released(red_button)
<END_OF_PLAN>
